adds.rationale: Replace the narrow uranium-mining ETF (URA) with the Range Nuclear Renaissance ETF, which directly holds the nuclear power operators and SMR developers — Talen Energy, GE Vernova, Rolls-Royce — that are the commercial beneficiaries of the three AI-data-center nuclear PPAs signed in Q4 2024, aligning the nuclear slot with the profile's explicit 'SMRs and utility PPAs to AI data centers' thesis.

news_evidence.summary: Microsoft signs a 20-year PPA with Constellation to restart Three Mile Island Unit 1 (835 MW) as baseload for PJM data centers; Constellation projects higher EPS growth through 2030.
Google and Kairos Power sign the first US corporate Master Plant Development Agreement for 500 MW of SMR capacity by 2035 — the first multi-plant SMR PPA in US history.

NUKZ launched January 23, 2024 and gained ~70% through year-end; top holdings include Cameco (supply), Talen Energy (operator), GE Vernova, and Rolls-Royce (SMR) — full-stack nuclear renaissance exposure vs URA's mining-only coverage.

Google Willow 105-qubit chip achieves below-threshold error correction (Dec 9), validating the quantum thesis behind IONQ and underscoring simultaneous hyperscaler capex across AI, quantum, and nuclear.

removes.rationale: URA's holdings are uranium miners (Cameco, Kazatomprom) capturing the fuel supply chain, not the nuclear operators and SMR developers that are the direct counterparties in the Q4 2024 hyperscaler PPAs; NUKZ supersedes URA with Cameco as its largest holding plus the operators and reactor designers URA lacks.

news_evidence.summary: AWS acquired Talen's Cumulus Data campus for $650M and secured a carbon-free PPA from Talen's Susquehanna nuclear plant — value flows to operator Talen (in NUKZ, absent from URA).
FERC rejected the Talen-PJM interconnection amendment 2-1, blocking expansion of co-located nuclear load — regulatory action landed on operators (Talen, PJM), not uranium miners.

Uranium spot retreated from a ~$107/lb early-2024 peak to ~$80/lb by year-end while nuclear operator stocks surged on PPA news — the fuel cycle and the operator PPA cycle have de-coupled, making URA the wrong instrument for the active thesis.

rationale_overall: Q4 2024 produced three landmark hyperscaler-nuclear PPAs — Microsoft/Constellation TMI (Sept), AWS/Talen Susquehanna (Mar deal, Oct expansion attempt), and Google/Kairos SMR fleet (Oct) — establishing nuclear operators and SMR developers as the commercial center of gravity in the nuclear wave, not uranium mining. URA is the mining layer only; NUKZ holds both the mining layer (Cameco) and the operator/reactor layer (Talen, GE Vernova, Rolls-Royce). Swapping URA for NUKZ keeps the watchlist at 8/8 and upgrades the nuclear slot from fuel-supply-only to full-stack renaissance exposure. The other holdings stay thesis-intact: Google's Willow chip reinforces quantum (IONQ); Nvidia Blackwell shipments validate AI capex (MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA); RKLB's expanding launch cadence sustains the rockets thesis.
